skill.name: product-hunt-launch
skill.description: Guides Product Hunt launch planning with listing specs, gallery strategy, tagline formulas, maker comments, launch timing, and post-launch follow-up.
skill.summary: Plan a Product Hunt launch with practical copy, image, timing, research, and engagement guidance.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and network patterns in SKILL.md. Most Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives from Markdown examples and prose, but the documented curl-to-shell installer and external cloud app calls are real risks that require a user warning. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Piped Remote Installer in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Line 14 pipes a remote HTTPS installer into sh before login. This is a true positive because it encourages executing unaudited remote code, although no malicious payload is shown.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and security-sensitive. Confidence is below very high because the file describes a vendor installer and line 29 claims checksum verification.

medium_findings.title: External Cloud Services Receive Launch Data
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to run inference, image generation, and search apps through external services. This is legitimate for the skill, but prompts and research queries may expose product details.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands clearly invoke remote image and search providers through infsh. The use appears intentional and relevant, so the risk is privacy exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Findings Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick alerts at code fence and inline-code lines are false positives. SKILL.md is Markdown, and the actual tool allowance limits agent Bash use to infsh commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible Markdown fences or inline code, not Ruby execution. Some contained examples still need user caution, which is covered by separate findings.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain Product Hunt prose, prompt text, numeric listing guidance, or SHA-256 checksum language. No MD5, SHA-1, or weak cipher implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic code on the cited lines. The only checksum reference is SHA-256 verification, which is not a weak algorithm finding in this context.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines are a gallery prompt and launch timing advice. No command gathers host, user, process, file, or network configuration from the local system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contents are ordinary marketing guidance. There is no semantic evidence of local system discovery or exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Pipe to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start includes a curl-to-sh installer. Users should verify installer contents and checksums before running it, especially for a community skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern exactly matches remote script execution through a shell. The surrounding note reduces suspicion but does not remove the execution risk.

content.value_statement: Product Hunt launches need clear copy, strong visuals, timing, and fast engagement. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical launch checklist and content framework.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes Product Hunt listing requirements for names, taglines, topics, links, and gallery images.
Provides gallery image planning with recommended positions, dimensions, and example image prompts.
Gives tagline formulas and examples for clear launch positioning.
Drafts a maker comment structure for the first launch reply.
Provides a launch day timeline and engagement rules.
Suggests post-launch follow-up actions and common mistake fixes.
limitations: Product Hunt rules and image specifications may change, so users should verify current platform requirements.
The skill does not guarantee ranking, upvotes, traffic, or Product of the Day results.
External search and image generation commands may send prompts or product details to third-party services.
The installation example uses a remote shell installer that users should verify before execution.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Startup Launch
use_cases.description: Build a clear listing, gallery plan, maker comment, and launch day schedule before publishing.

use_cases.title: Improve a Side Project Listing
use_cases.description: Turn rough product notes into a concise tagline, focused topics, and better gallery image ideas.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Launch Assets
use_cases.description: Create a shared checklist for visuals, copy, supporter outreach, and post-launch follow-up.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Launch Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a Product Hunt launch checklist for my product. Ask for any missing product details first.

prompt_templates.title: Write Tagline Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to generate 12 Product Hunt taglines under 60 characters for my product and explain the strongest three.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Gallery Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan five Product Hunt gallery images for my product, including the goal and visual brief for each image.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Launch Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit my Product Hunt launch plan, identify weak points, and produce a prioritized 7-day action plan.

output_examples.input: A founder asks for a launch plan for an AI analytics tool.
output_examples.output: A launch checklist with tagline options, recommended topics, five gallery image briefs, maker comment outline, and launch day timeline.

output_examples.input: A team wants to improve weak Product Hunt gallery images.
output_examples.output: A revised gallery sequence that starts with product value, then shows the core workflow, proof, integrations, and next step.

output_examples.input: A marketer asks what to avoid during launch day.
output_examples.output: A risk list covering asking for upvotes, slow comment replies, vague taglines, generic screenshots, and poor timing.

best_practices: Verify current Product Hunt rules, image sizes, and terms before launch day.
Replace example prompts and comments with real product details and truthful claims.
Review any remote installer or external service command before running it.
anti_patterns: Do not ask people to upvote your launch directly.
Do not publish generic visuals that fail to show the actual product.
Do not paste sensitive roadmap, customer, or credential data into external research tools.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create Product Hunt launch copy, gallery plans, launch day checklists, maker comments, and follow-up actions.

faq.question: Can it guarantee a top Product Hunt ranking?
faq.answer: No. It improves preparation and execution, but rankings depend on product fit, audience, timing, and platform behavior.

faq.question: Does it generate images automatically?
faq.answer: It includes example infsh commands for image generation, but users must run and review those commands separately.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private launch details?
faq.answer: Use caution. External search and image services may receive prompts, queries, product names, or positioning details.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I verify before launch?
faq.answer: Verify Product Hunt rules, image dimensions, topic limits, launch timing, and any installer or external service command.
